title: Pritzker Traubert Foundation Intern 2026 in Chicago, USA | Paid Social Impact Internship at $20/Hour
summary: The Pritzker Traubert Foundation Intern 2026 opportunity is now open for applications. If you are passionate about creating economic opportunity, supporting communities, and gaining hands-on experience in the nonprofit sector, this paid internship in Chicago could be the perfect next step. The Pritzker Traubert Foundation works to strengthen Chicago through partnerships with visionary leaders focused on connecting talent to jobs, expanding access to capital, and building an inclusive economy where all neighborhoods can thrive. This internship offers practical experience, professional development, and the chance to contribute to meaningful projects that create lasting impact. Why Apply for the Pritzker Traubert Foundation Internship? This internship is ideal for students, graduates, and aspiring professionals who want to build real-world experience in philanthropy, nonprofit leadership, and community investment. Interns will support both operational and programmatic initiatives while working closely with professionals dedicated to social change. Internship Benefits Paid position: $20 per hour Flexible schedule: 20–24 hours per week Hybrid work model: Office and remote work combination Professional experience: Exposure to nonprofit operations and program strategy Meaningful impact: Work on projects related to workforce, democracy, and community investment Networking opportunities: Learn from experienced leaders in the philanthropic sector Flexible duration: 8 to 10 weeks depending on availability Work Schedule Core working hours include: Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Office) Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Remote) Thursday: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM (Office) Additional flexibility may be available depending on tasks and weekly priorities. Key Responsibilities Selected interns will: Support projects in community investment, workforce, and democracy Assist with internal operations and administration Help develop communications materials and special projects Organize and track ongoing tasks and priorities Contribute to the foundation’s day-to-day strategic work Preferred Qualifications Applicants should demonstrate: Passion for social impact and community development Strong attention to detail Excellent written communication skills Ability to work independently and collaboratively Flexibility to take on new assignments Strong organizational skills Experience using Microsoft Office Suite Internship Timeline Start Date: June 2026 (Flexible) End Date: August 2026 (Flexible) Application Deadline May 8, 2026 How to Apply Send your resume and cover letter with the subject line “Intern” to: vreyes@ptfound.org APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more internship opportunities, click here . Why This Opportunity Stands Out Paid internships in philanthropy and community development are highly competitive. This role gives you the opportunity to gain professional experience while contributing to initiatives that directly improve lives and neighborhoods in Chicago. If you want to build your resume, develop leadership skills, and make a real impact, this internship is worth applying for. title: Paradigm Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Retreat in Northern California for Technical Builders Worldwide
summary: The Paradigm Fellowship 2026 is now accepting applications from ambitious technical builders around the world. This highly competitive, fully funded four-day retreat in Northern California is designed for students and early-career innovators working across cutting-edge fields such as artificial intelligence, robotics, energy, aerospace, bioengineering, crypto, prediction markets, and more. If you are deeply technical, passionate about solving hard problems, and looking to connect with exceptional peers, this fellowship could be a career-defining opportunity. What is the Paradigm Fellowship? The Paradigm Fellowship is an immersive retreat that brings together talented builders, researchers, and problem-solvers from around the world. It creates an environment where participants can collaborate, exchange ideas, and build relationships with some of the most promising minds in technology. Previous fellows have included undergraduates, graduate students, dropouts, and alumni from top organizations such as OpenAI, SpaceX, Citadel, and Kalshi. Many past participants have gone on to: Meet cofounders Launch startups Join research teams Raise funding for their ideas Access new career opportunities Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive full financial support, including: Travel funding Lodging accommodation Meals during the retreat Access to exclusive fireside chats with top builders Collaborative whiteboarding sessions Dedicated time to build, hack, and experiment Networking with a curated community of ambitious innovators Program Details Program Name: Paradigm Fellowship 2026 Location: Northern California Dates: August 12–15, 2026 Funding Type: Fully Funded Who Should Apply? Paradigm is looking for individuals who demonstrate strong technical ability and high potential for growth. There is no strict age requirement. Ideal applicants may include: High school students with exceptional technical projects University students in STEM fields Graduate researchers Engineers and developers Builders creating innovative products Technical professionals early in their careers Founders and aspiring entrepreneurs What matters most is not your title, but what you have built, shipped, or figured out. Why You Should Apply The Paradigm Fellowship is more than a retreat. It is a gateway to future collaborations, mentorship, and long-term opportunities. If you are serious about building impactful technology and want to be surrounded by world-class thinkers, this is the kind of room that can accelerate your journey. Application Deadline Deadline: June 8, 2026 Final decisions for selected fellows are expected by July 1, 2026 . How to Apply Submit your application through the official link below: APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts For young technical talent seeking an elite, fully funded opportunity in 2026, the Paradigm Fellowship stands out as one of the best programs available. Whether your passion is AI, robotics, energy, aerospace, or frontier innovation, this fellowship offers the community, resources, and inspiration to help you level up. Apply before the deadline and take your next big step. title: RAISEAfrica 2026 Open Call: Win Funding, Mentorship & Global Exposure for Renewable Energy Startups in Africa
summary: Young African entrepreneurs and renewable energy innovators now have a powerful opportunity to grow their ideas into scalable businesses. Applications are officially open for RAISEAfrica 2026 – Renewables Accelerators for Innovative Startups and Entrepreneurs in Africa. Launched by the RES4Africa Foundation, the programme is designed to support the next generation of startups and entrepreneurs driving Africa’s clean energy future. Through expert guidance, funding opportunities, and access to industry leaders, RAISEAfrica 2026 helps innovators transform renewable energy ideas into real-world impact. Implemented with the support of Enel Green Power and the European Investment Bank (EIB), and in collaboration with EY Foundation, EDP Renewables, CSIR, and SAICA, this initiative offers a tailored growth journey for selected participants. Why RAISEAfrica 2026 Matters Africa’s renewable energy sector continues to expand rapidly, creating space for bold entrepreneurs to solve pressing energy challenges. However, many founders need funding, mentorship, visibility, and strategic partnerships to scale successfully. RAISEAfrica bridges that gap by giving young innovators direct access to tools, networks, and resources that can accelerate their businesses and increase their impact across the continent. Benefits of RAISEAfrica 2026 Selected participants will receive access to a structured pre-acceleration and acceleration programme that includes: Expert mentoring and personalized coaching Networking with industry leaders and investors International visibility and exposure Fundraising support and investor readiness guidance Connections with major players in the energy ecosystem Opportunities to participate in global events Funding Opportunities Up to 6 startups or entrepreneurs will be selected for the programme. Each selected participant receives: Entry contribution of €3,000 Opportunity to compete for the Young Talent of the Year Award Final prize ranging from €5,000 to €10,000 Who Can Apply for RAISEAfrica 2026? To be eligible, applicants must: Be between 18 and 35 years old Be an African citizen or represent a startup registered in Africa Develop renewable energy solutions for the African market Demonstrate proficiency in English Applicants with projects ranging from early-stage ideas to advanced startup models are welcome to apply. Selection Criteria Applications will be reviewed based on: Innovation and originality of the solution Relevance to Africa’s renewable energy market Potential impact Strength of business model Scalability Entrepreneur’s motivation and commitment The programme also promotes gender balance and strongly encourages women entrepreneurs to apply. Application Deadline All applications must be submitted by: May 17, 2026 – 11:59 PM CET Late submissions may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to apply early. How to Apply Interested candidates should complete the official application form and review the full call for applications through the official programme platform. For inquiries: giovanni.formicola@res4africa.org APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts RAISEAfrica 2026 is more than an accelerator—it is a launchpad for Africa’s renewable energy future. If you are a young founder building solutions in clean energy, this is your chance to gain mentorship, funding, and access to influential networks that can take your startup to the next level. Apply now and position your innovation at the center of Africa’s energy transformation. title: Fully Funded IHE Delft Scholarship 2025–2026 for Africa and MENA
summary: If you are searching for a fully funded international master’s scholarship in water and sustainable development, the IHE Delft Sahel, MENA, and Horn of Africa Scholarship Programme is one of the most prestigious and impactful opportunities available today. Designed to build the next generation of water professionals across Africa and the Middle East, this opportunity offers not only funding but also global exposure, technical expertise, and career advancement in a highly critical sector. About the Scholarship Programme The IHE Delft Water and Development Partnership Programme, funded by the Dutch Ministry of Foreign Affairs, aims to strengthen capacity and build a strong network of water professionals across developing regions. This initiative focuses on addressing global water challenges such as: Water scarcity Climate change impacts Sustainable water management Flood control and environmental resilience The programme is specifically tailored to support professionals from regions where water-related challenges are most urgent, including Africa and the Middle East. What the Scholarship Covers (Funding Benefits) Successful applicants can receive either full funding or partial funding. Fully Funded Scholarship Includes: Full tuition fees Health insurance International travel costs Monthly living allowance of approximately €1,220 This comprehensive package ensures that scholars can focus entirely on their academic and professional development without financial stress. Eligible Countries and Regions This scholarship is region-specific, targeting candidates from: 1. Horn of Africa Ethiopia Kenya Somalia South Sudan (Sudan and Uganda may be restricted depending on cycle) 2. Middle East (MENA) Egypt Iraq Jordan Palestine Yemen 3. Sahel Region Burkina Faso Mali Niger Nigeria These regions are prioritized due to their high vulnerability to water-related challenges and need for skilled professionals. Who Can Apply? (Target Audience) This opportunity is ideal for: Early-career and mid-career professionals Water sector practitioners Environmental scientists and engineers Development professionals working in water-related fields Individuals committed to returning and contributing to their home countries There is also a strong preference for female candidates and underrepresented groups, encouraging gender equity in water leadership. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must meet the following criteria: Academic Requirements Must gain admission into the MSc in Water and Sustainable Development Strong academic background Residency Nationality Must be a citizen of one of the eligible countries Must be living and/or working in the eligible region Professional Personal Criteria Demonstrated motivation and commitment Relevant work experience is an advantage Willingness to return to home country after graduation Programme Obligations Applicants must: Conduct MSc research in their home country or region Participate in programme seminars and knowledge networks Contribute to capacity-building initiatives Selection Criteria Candidates are evaluated based on: Academic excellence Strong motivation and clear career goals Relevant work experience Commitment to regional development Gender balance (priority given to women) Additionally, applicants who can secure co-funding from other sources may have an advantage in competitive cases. Application Process (Step-by-Step) Unlike many scholarships, this programme does not require a separate scholarship application. Step 1: Apply for Admission Submit your application to the MSc programme in Water and Sustainable Development Step 2: Receive Admission Offer Only admitted candidates are considered for the scholarship Step 3: Automatic Consideration If eligible, you will automatically be reviewed for the scholarship Step 4: Prepare Supporting Requirements English proficiency test (must be submitted before deadline) Motivation responses Academic documents Important: Failure to submit English test results before the deadline may result in disqualification. Apply here Why This Scholarship Matters Water is one of the most critical global challenges of the 21st century. This scholarship directly contributes to: Building sustainable water systems Strengthening resilience to climate change Supporting developing economies Creating global networks of water professionals By investing in education, the programme empowers scholars to become leaders and innovators in water management within their regions. Deadline 1st June 2026 The IHE Delft Sahel, MENA, and Horn of Africa Scholarship is more than just financial support—it is a career-transforming opportunity for professionals passionate about solving real-world water challenges. With full funding, international exposure, and a strong emphasis on regional impact, this scholarship stands out as one of the most valuable opportunities for candidates from Africa and the Middle East. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Fully Funded IHE Delft Scholarship 2025–2026 for Africa and MENA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded Arms Control Negotiation Academy (ACONA) Fellowship 2026–2027: Global Opportunity for International Security Experts
summary: 7If you are a professional or researcher seeking to build a high-impact career in international security, diplomacy, or global negotiations, the Arms Control Negotiation Academy (ACONA) Fellowship 2026–2027 presents one of the most prestigious and transformative opportunities currently open worldwide. This highly competitive program equips participants with advanced negotiation skills, deep policy insight, and access to a global network of leading experts in arms control and international affairs. What is the ACONA Fellowship? The Arms Control Negotiation Academy (ACONA) is a globally recognized, high-level professional development fellowship designed for emerging leaders in international security and diplomacy. The program brings together a select cohort of participants from around the world to engage in intensive training on arms control, negotiation strategies, and global security challenges. This fellowship stands out because it blends: Academic excellence Real-world negotiation simulations Collaboration with top experts and institutions Exposure to global geopolitical dynamics Participants gain both theoretical knowledge and hands-on experience, positioning them as future leaders in international negotiations and arms control policy. Why This Fellowship Matters in Today’s World Global security is undergoing rapid transformation due to rising geopolitical tensions, technological advancements, and evolving threats. The ACONA Fellowship addresses these challenges by training professionals to: Navigate complex international negotiations Understand nuclear and arms control frameworks Respond to emerging security risks Build collaborative solutions across nations The program emphasizes the urgent need for skilled negotiators capable of addressing modern global conflicts and security issues. Key Benefits of the ACONA Fellowship Participating in the ACONA Fellowship offers numerous professional and academic advantages: 1. Advanced Negotiation Training Fellows engage in immersive exercises and simulations that replicate real-world diplomatic negotiations. 2. Global Networking Opportunities Participants interact with leading scholars, policymakers, and practitioners from around the world. 3. Research and Collaboration Fellows work on joint research projects addressing pressing international security issues. 4. Certification Participants receive a Certificate in Arms Control Negotiation, enhancing their professional credentials. 5. Funded Participation The fellowship may cover travel and accommodation for key program components, including international boot camps and conferences. Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates) The ACONA Fellowship is open to applicants from all countries worldwide, making it a truly global opportunity. Target Audience This fellowship is ideal for: Early to mid-career professionals Researchers and scholars Policy analysts Diplomats and international relations experts Required Experience Applicants must demonstrate: 3–5 years of professional or research experience in relevant fields Fields of Eligibility Candidates must have a background in one or more of the following areas: Arms control and nuclear issues Negotiation and mediation International peace and security Global affairs (especially involving major powers such as the U.S., Russia, China, and the EU Additional Qualifications To stand out as a strong applicant, candidates are expected to have: An advanced academic degree (preferred) Demonstrated international experience Recognized achievements in their field Strong analytical and communication skills A clear commitment to global security and diplomacy Application Requirements To apply for the ACONA Fellowship, applicants must submit the following documents: 1. Motivation Letter (1–2 pages) This should clearly outline: Your passion for arms control and international security Your career goals What you will contribute to the ACONA community 2. Curriculum Vitae (CV/Resume) Maximum of 5 pages Must highlight relevant academic and professional experience 3. Letter of Recommendation Submitted directly by a referee via email Should emphasize your qualifications and achievements All documents must be submitted in PDF format through the official application portal. Apply here What Makes ACONA Highly Competitive? The ACONA Fellowship selects a small, elite cohort of participants globally, making it extremely competitive. Selection is based on: Professional excellence Academic achievements Leadership potential Commitment to international peace and security Applicants are encouraged to present a strong, well-structured application that demonstrates both expertise and impact. Career Opportunities After the Fellowship Graduates of the ACONA Fellowship gain access to a wide range of career pathways, including: International organizations (e.g., UN, NATO-related institutions) Government and diplomatic services Think tanks and research institutions Academic and policy advisory roles The program’s strong global network significantly enhances career advancement opportunities. Deadline 31st May 2026 The ACONA Fellowship 2026–2027 is more than just a professional program—it is a gateway to becoming a key player in global security and diplomacy. With its rigorous training, international exposure, and prestigious network, it offers a unique platform for professionals committed to shaping the future of international peace and arms control. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Fully Funded Arms Control Negotiation Academy (ACONA) Fellowship 2026–2027: Global Opportunity for International Security Experts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mama Cash Solidarity Fund 2026: A Fully Funded Global Grant Opportunity for Women’s Funds Advancing Feminist Movements Worldwide (Up to €40,000)
summary: The Mama Cash Solidarity Fund 2026 is a prestigious global funding opportunity designed to strengthen feminist movements by supporting women-led funding organizations across the world. Established by Mama Cash, this grant program plays a critical role in advancing human rights, gender justice, and social transformation by investing directly in women’s funds. Unlike many traditional grants, the Solidarity Fund is rooted in participatory grantmaking and collective decision-making. It is specifically created by women’s funds for women’s funds, making it a unique and highly impactful funding stream in the global development and human rights ecosystem. Funding Value and Duration Grant Amount: €30,000 – €40,000 Duration: 1 year This funding is designed to support internal development and sustainability rather than external regranting activities. Who Can Apply? (Eligible Categories of Applicants) The Mama Cash Solidarity Fund is highly specific in its eligibility requirements. Only organizations that qualify as women’s funds are eligible. Eligible Applicants Include: Women’s funds (primary requirement) Nonprofit organizations engaged in grantmaking Feminist funding organizations Emerging or newly established women’s funds Independent (or soon-to-be autonomous) funding entities Importantly, applicants do not need to be members of Prospera (the global network of women’s funds) to apply. Geographic Eligibility Eligible Countries: Worldwide Regions: Open to applicants from all continents This is a fully global opportunity, meaning organizations from Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, Oceania, and other regions can apply without geographic restriction. Core Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the Mama Cash Solidarity Fund, applicants must meet all of the following criteria: 1. Feminist Mission and Vision Organizations must operate from a feminist perspective. Their primary mission must focus on advancing the rights of: Women Girls Trans people Intersex people 2. Self-Led Structure The organization must be led by the communities it serves. Leadership and decision-making must include individuals directly affected by the issues addressed. 3. Women’s Fund Identity The organization must: Raise funds Provide grants to grassroots groups, collectives, or individuals Grantmaking must be a primary function, not a secondary activity. 4. Autonomy The organization must: Be independent from larger institutions Or have a clear plan to become autonomous within 2–3 years 5. Commitment to Social Change Applicants must demonstrate: A vision to challenge unjust power structures A strategy for long-term systemic transformation What the Grant Supports The Solidarity Fund is not designed for project-based funding or regranting, but instead focuses on strengthening the internal capacity of women’s funds. Examples of Fundable Activities: Leadership development and transitions Organizational learning and evaluation systems Staff wellbeing and collective care Financial sustainability strategies Digital security improvements Institutional strengthening and resilience building This makes the grant particularly valuable for organizations seeking to grow, stabilize, or restructure. What the Grant Does NOT Support Applicants should carefully note the exclusions: Regranting activities (covered under a different fund) Political parties or government agencies Religious institutions Organizations that restrict or deny rights of marginalized groups Anti-rights or discriminatory initiatives Unique Features of the Solidarity Fund 1. Participatory Grantmaking Model Unlike traditional grants, applicants can also become reviewers. Eligible women’s funds participate in evaluating proposals and allocating funding. 2. Peer-Led Decision Making Applicants help decide which organizations receive funding, creating a transparent and equitable system. 3. Focus on Movement Building The fund prioritizes: Collaboration Solidarity across regions Strengthening feminist ecosystems globally 4. Emphasis on Trust and Equity The process is guided by: Radical trust Collective accountability Shared power Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on: Organizational Strengthening Potential: How the grant will improve capacity and sustainability Relevance and Urgency: The importance of the proposed work in its context Movement Impact: Contribution to broader feminist and social justice movements Application Process Step-by-Step Guide: Complete the official application form (or submit a video application) Prepare a detailed budget Submit via email during the application window Optionally participate in the peer review proces Await final selection and ranking results Applications are accepted in multiple languages, including English, French, and Spanish. Apply here Why You Should Apply The Mama Cash Solidarity Fund is not just a grant—it is a strategic investment in feminist leadership and global justice movements. Key Benefits: Substantial funding (€30,000–€40,000) Global recognition and credibility Opportunity to collaborate with international feminist networks Participation in decision-making processes Strengthening long-term organizational sustainability Deadline 30th April 2026 The Mama Cash Solidarity Fund 2026 stands out as one of the most impactful global grant opportunities for feminist organizations. Its emphasis on community leadership, autonomy, and systemic change makes it ideal for women’s funds seeking to deepen their impact and resilience. If your organization is committed to advancing the rights of women, girls, trans, and intersex people while strengthening feminist movements globally, this opportunity offers both financial support and a powerful platform for growth. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Mama Cash Solidarity Fund 2026: A Fully Funded Global Grant Opportunity for Women’s Funds Advancing Feminist Movements Worldwide (Up to €40,000) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Global Development Awards Competition 2026: Win Up to $50,000+ in Funding for Research & Development Projects
summary: The Global Development Network Global Development Awards Competition 2026 (GDAC 2026) is one of the most prestigious global opportunities for researchers, NGOs, and development practitioners seeking funding, recognition, and international exposure. If you are passionate about solving real-world development challenges, this competition offers a powerful platform to scale your ideas and impact vulnerable communities across the globe. What is the Global Development Awards Competition? The Global Development Awards Competition (GDAC) is a flagship global initiative designed to: Identify and support innovative development ideas Fund impactful research and social projects Promote evidence-based policymaking Strengthen research capacity in developing countries It brings together researchers, NGOs, policymakers, and development practitioners working to improve lives in underserved and vulnerable communities worldwide . At its core, GDAC focuses on solutions that empower communities, reduce inequality, and promote sustainable development. Categories Under GDAC 2026 The 2026 competition features two main award tracks: 1. Japanese Award for Outstanding Research on Development (ORD) Focuses on policy-oriented research Supports researchers generating actionable insights Encourages innovation in science, technology, and development 2. Japanese Award for Most Innovative Development Project (MIDP) Targets NGOs and civil society organizations Funds ongoing development projects Emphasizes scalability and real-world impact Both categories are designed to address pressing global challenges such as poverty, inequality, sustainability, and human security. Key Theme for 2026 The 2026 edition focuses on: “Science, Technology, and Innovation (STI) for Peace and Human Security” This theme encourages applicants to explore how innovation can: Promote peacebuilding Strengthen resilience Improve livelihoods Address global crises Funding, Benefits, and Rewards Winning GDAC 2026 participants gain access to: Financial Grants Up to $50,000 for top projects Additional prizes (e.g., $25,000 for second place) Technical Support Mentorship from experienced development experts Guidance throughout project implementation Global Exposure Opportunity to present at international forums Recognition among global development leaders Networking Opportunities Access to a global community of researchers and practitioners Collaboration opportunities across countries Who Can Apply? Eligible Applicants For ORD (Research Category): Researchers in low- and middle-income countries Early-career and senior researchers Academics, policy analysts, and think-tank professionals For MIDP (Project Category): Non-profit organizations (NGOs) Civil society organizations (CSOs) Development practitioners with ongoing projects Eligible Countries and Regions Applicants are primarily expected from: Africa Asia Latin America Middle East Other low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) The competition strongly prioritizes participants working in developing and transition economies. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for GDAC 2026, applicants must: Work on projects addressing development challenges Focus on vulnerable and marginalized populations Demonstrate innovation and scalability Align with the 2026 theme (STI for Peace and Human Security) Show clear impact potential and sustainability Projects should aim to: Improve livelihoods Empower communities Deliver measurable outcomes Why You Should Apply GDAC is not just another funding opportunity—it is a career-defining platform. Here’s why: 1. Global Recognition Winners gain international visibility and credibility. 2. Real Impact Projects directly improve lives in underserved communities. 3. Career Advancement Researchers and practitioners gain access to global networks and future opportunities. 4. Funding + Mentorship Unlike many grants, GDAC combines funding with expert guidance. How to Apply Applicants must: Visit the official GDAC 2026 page Choose the appropriate category (ORD or MIDP) Prepare a strong proposal aligned with the theme Submit before the deadline Ensure your application clearly demonstrates: Problem statement Innovative solution Implementation plan Expected impact Apply here Deadline 17th June 2026 The Global Development Awards Competition 2026 stands out as one of the most impactful global opportunities for individuals and organizations committed to driving change. With substantial funding, global recognition, and expert mentorship, it empowers participants to transform ideas into real-world solutions. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Global Development Awards Competition 2026: Win Up to $50,000+ in Funding for Research Development Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Racial Equity Research Grants – Call for Proposals
summary: Researchers are invited to apply for the Racial Equity Research Grants program, which supports academic research aimed at understanding and addressing racial inequality in education. About the Grant This grant funds rigorous academic research projects that contribute to advancing equity within education systems. The focus is on generating evidence that helps: Understand racial disparities in education Inform policies and interventions Promote equitable outcomes for marginalized communities What the Grant Supports Eligible proposals must: Be focused on education (broadly defined) Include a strong research component Demonstrate clear academic rigor and relevance What Is Not Eligible The program does not support : Program evaluations without a research focus Professional development activities Curriculum development alone Scholarships or capital projects Research outside the field of education Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for: Researchers with a doctoral degree (PhD or equivalent) Academics and scholars in education or related fields Individuals working on racial equity and inclusion in education Key Details Opportunity: Racial Equity Research Grants Focus Area: Racial inequality in education Eligibility: Doctorate holders Application: Open (check link for deadlines and details) Why This Opportunity Matters This grant supports research that can shape policies and practices to reduce racial inequality in education , making it a powerful opportunity for scholars committed to social justice and systemic change. title: Fully Fund Your Career Growth: SIPRI Internships 2026 in Stockholm (Global Opportunity for Future Peace Leaders)
summary: If you are passionate about global peace, international relations, and development, the 2026 internship opportunities at the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute offer a unique chance to gain hands-on experience in one of the world’s most respected research institutions. Based in Solna, Stockholm, these internships are designed for motivated students and recent graduates eager to contribute to peacebuilding, policy, and global development efforts. SIPRI is globally recognized for its research on conflict, security, and peace. Established in 1966, the institute provides critical data, analysis, and policy recommendations to governments, researchers, and international stakeholders. Overview of SIPRI Internships 2026 SIPRI is offering five internship positions running from mid-July/August to mid-December 2026 , across three dynamic teams: 1. Communication Team (2 Positions) Interns in this team will: Support the promotion of SIPRI’s research, publications, and events Handle media inquiries and assist with communications strategies Contribute to social media content and multimedia production Support the 2026 Stockholm Forum on Peace and Development communication efforts Assist in launching SIPRI’s major databases You will collaborate closely with internal communication teams and external partners, gaining real-world experience in media and digital engagement. 2. Director’s Office (1 Position) This role offers exposure to high-level decision-making and leadership processes. Key responsibilities include: Supporting internal planning and coordination Assisting in high-level meetings and engagements Drafting speeches, notes, and talking points Organizing board meetings, events, and official visits This is a fast-paced environment ideal for those interested in governance, policy, and executive support. 3. Stockholm Forum Team (2 Positions) Interns will help organize the prestigious Stockholm Forum on Peace and Development , scheduled for 3–4 November 2026 . Responsibilities include: Supporting event planning and logistics Coordinating with global participants and stakeholders Assisting in delivering a large-scale international conference Providing on-site support during the forum This forum gathers policymakers, researchers, activists, and experts worldwide to foster dialogue on peace and development. Key Responsibilities Across Internships All interns will engage in a variety of tasks such as: Administrative and operational support Information management and coordination Communication with stakeholders Meeting participation and note-taking Event logistics and travel coordination Content creation for digital platforms (especially for communication interns) Eligibility Criteria and Requirements To qualify, applicants must: Be enrolled in a university internship program or affiliated with an organization Have valid insurance and a designated supervisor/contact person Be currently studying or recently graduated in fields such as: International Relations Political Science Development Studies Communication Management Additional Requirements: Strong interest in peace, conflict, and development issues Experience in coordination, administration, or event planning is an advantage Excellent command of English (Swedish is a plus) Strong organizational, communication, and teamwork skills Proficiency in tools like Microsoft Office, Adobe Creative Suite, and social media platforms What You Will Gain Interning at SIPRI provides a rich learning experience, including: Hands-on experience in international policy and operations Exposure to global peacebuilding and development processes Participation in organizing a major international conference Skills in multimedia communication and digital content creation Professional networking in a multicultural environment Mentorship and structured learning support Internship Conditions Location: Stockholm, Sweden (with occasional remote work possibility) Duration: Mid-July/August to mid-December 2026 Type: Full-time Compensation: Unpaid (no coverage for travel, housing, or other expenses) Supervision: Dedicated mentor assigned Application Deadline The final date to apply is: Sunday, 10 May 2026 (23:59) Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews in mid-to-late May 2026. Why Choose SIPRI? SIPRI is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity across gender, nationality, and cultural backgrounds. Interning here means joining a globally respected institution that actively shapes policies and discussions around peace and security. How to Apply Interested candidates can apply directly via the official SIPRI application portal: click here. Learn more about this opportunity on the official website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more Internship opportunities? Visit the OFY website: Click here .
